parsed.clarification_question: Is the requested review intended to be a substantive technical review including logic, evidence, novelty, and scholarship, or a lighter review focused on writing style, structure, and flow?

rationale.R1.2: Logical consistency is central to the review as it involves verifying the argument chain in the proposed architecture and evaluation.
rationale.R1.3: Evidence-claim alignment is central because the review should check if the reported results support the claims of reduced failure rates and improved naturalness.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty assessment is central to evaluate the delta of LMCR against existing systems in language-driven robot control.
rationale.R1.5: Structural reorganization is central for suggesting improvements to the section's ordering and narrative arc describing the architecture and evaluation.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is central to improve sentence-level grammar, flow, and conciseness in the technical description.
rationale.R1.7: Citation and scholarship is central to ensure accurate referencing and coverage of relevant literature on robot teleoperation and stability.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is central in the review to identify if limitations or variabilities in the evaluation are properly acknowledged.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is central to check if the results and expectations for transferability are stated without exaggeration.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is central to confirm the section stays focused on the LMCR architecture and its evaluation without unrelated digressions.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is central to flag any potential misuse of the robot control system that could lead to physical or safety issues.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability is central to verify the origins of the methods, such as the PD controller and threshold tuning, for reproducibility.
